description: Fix two browser-context bugs in the plugin SDK: (1) postFormData passes user-provided Content-Type headers through to fetch, breaking multipart boundary auto-generation, and (2) setLocalStorage/removeLocalStorage silently drop writes when localStorage is undefined (e.g., Discord) because they lack the iframe fallback that getLocalStorage has.

userStories.title: Strip Content-Type from user headers in postFormData
userStories.description: postFormData spreads all user-provided headers into the fetch call, including Content-Type. When Content-Type is present, it overrides the browser's auto-set multipart/form-data with boundary string, causing the server to receive a body that doesn't match the Content-Type. Delete Content-Type from extraHeaders before passing to fetchJSONImpl.
acceptanceCriteria: postFormData deletes content-type from extraHeaders before passing to fetchJSONImpl
When init contains headers with Content-Type, the actual fetch request uses the browser's auto-generated multipart/form-data Content-Type with boundary (not the user-provided value)
Non-Content-Type user headers (e.g., Authorization, X-Custom) are still forwarded correctly
A test verifies that user-provided Content-Type header is stripped and the browser auto-sets multipart/form-data with boundary

userStories.title: Add iframe fallback to setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage
userStories.description: getLocalStorage has an iframe fallback for environments where window.localStorage is undefined (e.g., Discord deletes the property). setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage lack this fallback, causing writes to be silently dropped with no warning while reads succeed. Add the same iframe fallback pattern to both functions.
acceptanceCriteria: setLocalStorage uses an iframe fallback to write when window.localStorage is undefined, matching the pattern in getLocalStorage
removeLocalStorage uses an iframe fallback to remove keys when window.localStorage is undefined
Both functions log a warning via log.warn when falling back to the iframe path (so plugin authors can see when this happens)
Both functions still return silently (no throw) if the iframe fallback itself fails
A test verifies setLocalStorage writes via iframe when localStorage is undefined, and the value can be read back via getLocalStorage's iframe fallback
A test verifies removeLocalStorage removes keys via iframe when localStorage is undefined
